text.title: finops-devops-cost-rightsizing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for finops-devops-cost-rightsizing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Rightsizing databases by comparing CPU utilization alone without checking connection count, IOPS, and buffer pool hit rate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A database may show 5% CPU but use 95% of provisioned IOPS or hold all data in buffer pool at the current memory size. Downsizing causes latency spikes that CPU metrics do not predict.

failure-mode.description: Running rightsizing recommendations directly from cloud provider tools without a staging validation step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provider tools optimize for cost, not performance headroom. AWS Compute Optimizer p99 recommendation may still saturate under a real traffic spike. Always validate in staging first.
